steps.title: Fastest path: mark the figure first
steps.content: Mark the given angle relationships on the figure first: equal angles, vertical angles, supplementary angles, parallel-line angles, and triangle sums of $180^\circ$. Most of these questions become a short angle chase once the givens are marked.

steps.title: Find the interior angle at R using the linear pair
steps.content:
Since $S$, $R$, and $Q$ are collinear, angles $\angle QRP$ and $\angle PRS$ form a linear pair on the line and are supplementary.
$$\angle QRP + \angle PRS = 180^{\circ}$$
$$\angle QRP = 180^{\circ} - 163^{\circ} = 17^{\circ}$$

steps.title: Apply the angle sum property to find $\angle QPR$
steps.content:
The sum of all angles in triangle $PQR$ equals $180^{\circ}$:
$$\angle QPR + \angle PQR + \angle QRP = 180^{\circ}$$
$$\angle QPR + 132^{\circ} + 17^{\circ} = 180^{\circ}$$
$$\angle QPR = 31^{\circ}$$
That matches Choice B — $31^\circ$.
